ontology.foodon_gap_note: No FoodOn term exists specifically for patlican salata (roasted eggplant salad with capsicum, garlic, parsley, mint, lemon and olive oil). FOODON_03311737 (eggplant food product) is used as the closest parent category.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 code A00LC (Aubergines/eggplants) is the closest defensible parent. No specific FoodEx2 code exists for roasted eggplant salad preparations.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No SNOMED CT concept found for patlican salata or roasted eggplant salad as a prepared dish.

preparation_method: dressed with olive oil and lemon juice

serving.natural_language_portion: One typical mezze serving of patlican salata is approximately 150 g, served chilled alongside bread as a starter or side dish.

nutrient_source.title: Baba Ghanoush / Roasted Eggplant Salad — USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy and Foundation Foods composite; eggplant (cooked), olive oil, capsicum, garlic, lemon juice
nutrient_source.organization: USDA Agricultural Research Service — FoodData Central
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: No exact USDA entry exists for patlican salata. Nutrient values are estimated from a weighted composite of USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy entries for the primary ingredients: cooked eggplant (USDA FDC #169228), olive oil (USDA FDC #171413), roasted red capsicum (USDA FDC #170108), raw garlic (USDA FDC #169230), fresh parsley (USDA FDC #170416), and lemon juice (USDA FDC #167747). Approximate ingredient proportions by weight: eggplant 60%, capsicum 15%, olive oil 10%, garlic 3%, parsley 5%, lemon juice 5%, dried mint 2%. Values are per 100 g of the finished dish.

gi_source.title: Eggplant (aubergine), boiled — Glycemic Index Database
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for patlican salata as a composite dish. The GI of 15 is derived from the University of Sydney GI Database entry for cooked eggplant (GI approximately 15, low category). Eggplant is the dominant ingredient (~60% by weight). The addition of olive oil, lemon juice, capsicum, garlic, and parsley does not raise the GI; fat and acid content from olive oil and lemon juice further attenuate glycemic response. Glycemic load per 150 g serving = (15 × 5.8 × 1.50) / 100 = 1.3 g, confirming very low GL.

hypertension.key_factor: Sodium content from seasoning and preparation
hypertension.guidance: Patlican salata contains approximately 180 mg sodium per 100 g; individuals with hypertension should monitor portion size and request reduced-salt preparation, while benefiting from the potassium (~230 mg/100 g) and olive oil content that support vascular health.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Very low glycemic index and high dietary fibre
type2_diabetes.guidance: With a glycemic index of approximately 15 and a glycemic load of only 1.3 per serving, patlican salata is highly suitable for individuals with Type 2 Diabetes; the dietary fibre (2.1 g/100 g) and olive oil content slow gastric emptying and blunt postprandial glucose excursions.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: High monounsaturated fat from olive oil and zero cholesterol
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Patlican salata is rich in monounsaturated fatty acids (~3.7 g/100 g) from olive oil, contains zero cholesterol, and provides antioxidant compounds from eggplant, capsicum, and parsley, making it a cardioprotective choice consistent with Mediterranean dietary patterns.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Patlican Salata (Turkish: Patlıcan Salatası) is a traditional Middle Eastern and Turkish roasted eggplant salad served as a cold mezze dish. The dish is prepared by roasting whole eggplants (aubergines) until the skin chars and the flesh becomes soft and smoky, then peeling and mixing the flesh with roasted or raw capsicum (bell pepper), finely chopped garlic, fresh parsley, dried mint, lemon juice, and extra-virgin olive oil. The result is a chunky, flavourful salad with a smoky, tangy, and herbaceous character. It is typically served chilled alongside flatbread or pita as a starter or side dish in Turkish, Levantine, and broader Middle Eastern cuisine. Nutritionally, patlican salata is a low-calorie, low-glycaemic dish (~72 kcal/100 g) dominated by vegetables and olive oil. It provides modest protein (1.4 g/100 g), predominantly monounsaturated fat (3.7 g/100 g), low carbohydrate (5.8 g/100 g), and useful dietary fibre (2.1 g/100 g). The olive oil contributes vitamin E and heart-healthy fatty acids. Capsicum and parsley contribute vitamin C (~18 mg/100 g) and folate. The dish is naturally vegan, gluten-free, and dairy-free. Its very low glycaemic index (~15) and low glycaemic load make it appropriate for individuals managing Type 2 Diabetes, and its monounsaturated fat profile supports cardiovascular health. Sodium content (~180 mg/100 g) warrants attention for hypertensive individuals. In Singapore, this dish is served at Middle Eastern restaurants such as Sahara Bar and Restaurant.

suitable as a light vegetarian dish

confidence_reason: No exact entry for patlican salata exists in any approved primary regional or global nutrient database. Nutrient values are estimated from a weighted ingredient composite using USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy entries for the individual components. GI is estimated by analogy from the University of Sydney GI Database entry for cooked eggplant. Confidence is therefore medium rather than high.
notes: Nutrient values are estimated from a weighted composite of USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy ingredient entries; no exact dish-level entry exists in HPB Singapore Foodo, ICMR-NIN, FAO/INFOODS SEA, or USDA for patlican salata as a prepared dish. Sodium estimate (180 mg/100 g) reflects moderate seasoning typical of restaurant preparation; home-prepared versions may vary. trans_fat_g is 0.0 (confirmed zero — olive oil and vegetable ingredients contain no industrial trans fats; naturally zero by ingredient composition). vitamin_b12_mcg is 0.0 (naturally zero — this is a fully plant-based dish with no animal-derived ingredients). vitamin_d_mcg is 0.0 (naturally zero — no animal-derived or fortified ingredients are present). cholesterol_mg is 0.0 (naturally zero — all ingredients are plant-based). monounsaturated_fat_g, polyunsaturated_fat_g, potassium_mg, calcium_mg, iron_mg, vitamin_c_mg, vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_e_mg, phosphorus_mg, magnesium_mg, zinc_mg, selenium_mcg, and water_g are estimated from USDA SR Legacy ingredient compositing and are not -1 sentinel values. GI of 15 is an analogous estimate from University of Sydney GI Database (cooked eggplant); no direct GI measurement for the composite dish was found in the approved GI literature. This is a Middle Eastern / Turkish dish, not a Singaporean dish; therefore the Singaporean multilingual naming requirements (Chinese, Malay, Tamil, Hindi) do not apply. Romanized Turkish name (Patlıcan Salatası) and Arabic aliases are provided for regional coverage. The dish is served at Sahara Bar and Restaurant, 58 Boat Quay, Singapore (field survey data source record GD000885).
